expectations.text: The output fetches data from the Fireblocks Greenhouse API endpoint (boards-api.greenhouse.io/v1/boards/fireblocks/jobs)
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows the agent used curl to fetch 'https://www.fireblocks.com/careers/' and 'https://boards.greenhouse.io/fireblocks' (the HTML board page), not the JSON API endpoint 'boards-api.greenhouse.io/v1/boards/fireblocks/jobs'. The agent scraped the HTML page with grep and Python parsing rather than using the dedicated API.

expectations.text: Results include specific job titles (not just 'jobs available')
expectations.evidence: Results.md lists 9 specific job titles with detailed information, e.g., 'Technical Integration Engineer', 'Senior Software Engineer, Transaction Security', 'Senior Backend Engineer, Key Management', 'Senior C++ Engineer, Key Management', etc.

expectations.text: Results include apply URLs or links for individual positions
expectations.evidence: Every listed position includes a Greenhouse apply URL, e.g., 'https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/fireblocks/jobs/4656817006' for the Technical Integration Engineer, and similar URLs for all 9 positions.

expectations.text: Results mention location information for jobs
expectations.evidence: All positions include 'Location: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Israel' or 'Department' information. The Senior Compliance Associate notes 'Tel Aviv-Yafo, Israel' in the Legal & Compliance department.

expectations.text: Output is formatted as a readable list or table (not raw JSON)
expectations.evidence: Results are formatted as a numbered Markdown list with structured fields (Location, Department, Published, Apply, Note) under each job title. No raw JSON in output.

claims.evidence: The with_skill run (same date) found 65 positions via the API. The without_skill run found 50 via HTML scraping. The discrepancy suggests the HTML page may not expose all positions, or the scraping missed some. The API data is likely more authoritative.

claims.claim: 9 Israel-based positions (8 in R&D, 1 in Legal & Compliance)
claims.evidence: The with_skill run found 17 Israel-based positions. The without_skill run found only 9. The HTML scraping approach missed 8 positions compared to the API, suggesting incomplete extraction.

claims.claim: Technical Integration Engineer is the best match for a new grad
claims.evidence: Both with_skill and without_skill runs agree that Technical Integration Engineer is the most promising role for a new grad, as it lacks the 'Senior' prefix.

eval_feedback.overall: The failed assertion correctly identifies the key difference: without the skill, the agent didn't know about the Greenhouse JSON API and instead scraped HTML, finding fewer jobs (50 vs 65). The assertions effectively differentiate skill-enabled vs. generic behavior.
